description: A designed horizontal 'Wall of Honor' memorial tribute card honoring a fallen officer. The background is a dark, muted slate/stone texture with a soft-focus American flag (stars and stripes) in the upper-right and a cluster of red roses along the right side, conveying solemn remembrance. Upper-left displays the white serif title 'Wall of Honor' next to an ICE/DHS memorial badge emblem wrapped in a blue-and-white mourning ribbon. Below that, white text reads: 'Customs Patrol Officer / George J. Spencer', a thin gold rule, 'End of Watch', another rule, and the date 'Monday, November 30, 1981'. The center-right features a large photograph of a gold law-enforcement badge inscribed 'U.S. CUSTOMS / THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY' and 'PATROL OFFICER' with number 271 and a central Treasury seal dated 1789. Style is a dignified, ceremonial memorial graphic. Messaging technique: institutional legacy-building and emotional appeal, honoring a historical line-of-duty death (per the tweet, Spencer was fatally shot in 1981 pursuing suspected car thieves) to tie modern ICE to a long heritage of sacrifice and public safety. Target audience: general public, law enforcement community, and ICE personnel.
notable_text: Wall of Honor; Customs Patrol Officer George J. Spencer; End of Watch; Monday, November 30, 1981; U.S. CUSTOMS THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY PATROL OFFICER
